settings.text: Greenfield started when Dan Novak traded a desk job for a pickup truck and a passion for working outdoors. Twenty years later, the company he built is a team of designers, arborists, and installers who share that same love of the land.

We've always believed that great landscaping starts with understanding the site — the soil, the light, the climate, and the people who live there. That's why every project begins with a conversation, not a catalog.

settings.name: Dan Novak
settings.bio: ISA-certified arborist with 20 years of hands-on experience. Dan designs every project personally and oversees installation from start to finish.

settings.name: Maria Santos
settings.bio: 12 years in the trade. Maria leads our installation crew and specializes in stone work, grading, and irrigation system design.

settings.name: Tyler Kim
settings.bio: Tyler manages our maintenance accounts and seasonal programs, ensuring every property stays healthy and sharp year-round.

settings.question: Are you licensed and insured?
settings.answer: Yes. Greenfield holds an active Oregon CCB contractor's license and carries full general liability and workers' compensation insurance. Our arborists hold ISA certification.

settings.question: What areas do you serve?
settings.answer: We work throughout the Portland metro area including SE, NE, NW Portland, Lake Oswego, Beaverton, Tigard, West Linn, and Sellwood. For larger commercial projects, we serve the broader Willamette Valley.

settings.question: How long does a typical project take?
settings.answer: Design takes 1-2 weeks after the initial consultation. Most residential installations complete in 1-3 weeks depending on scope. Hardscape-heavy projects may take longer. We always provide a clear timeline upfront.

settings.question: Do you offer maintenance plans?
settings.answer: Yes. We offer weekly, bi-weekly, and monthly maintenance programs that include mowing, pruning, weeding, fertilization, and seasonal cleanup. Plans are customized to your property's needs.

settings.question: When is the best time to start a project?
settings.answer: In Portland, spring (March-May) and fall (September-October) are ideal for planting. Hardscaping can happen year-round. We recommend booking design consultations 4-6 weeks before your target start date.
